sdk_code_examples.title: Delete a customer by ID
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

# Delete an existing customer by ID
client.customers.delete_customer(customer_id="cus_123456789")

sdk_code_examples.title: Retrieve a customer
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

customer = client.customers.get_customer(customer_id="cus_123456789")
print(customer.id, customer.email)

sdk_code_examples.title: Get order details
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

order = client.orders.get_order(order_id="ord_987654321", include=["items"]) 
print(order.id, order.status, len(order.items))

sdk_code_examples.title: Get order details (Java)
Create a new project
Create a new project (Python)
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

project = client.projects.create_project(
    name="My First Project",
    metadata={"env": "sandbox"},
)

print(project.id, project.name)

sdk_code_examples.title: List users with filters
Create a payment
Create a payment (Ruby)
sdk_code_examples.code:
require "acme_api"

client = AcmeAPI::Client.new(api_key: "sk_test_123")

payment = client.payments.create(
  amount: 4999,
  currency: "usd",
  customer_id: "cus_123456789"
)

puts "Created payment #{payment.id}"

sdk_code_examples.title: Retrieve an invoice
sdk_code_examples.code:
require "acme_api"

client = AcmeAPI::Client.new(api_key: "sk_test_123")

invoice = client.invoices.retrieve("inv_00001")

puts "Invoice ##{invoice.number} status=#{invoice.status} total=#{invoice.total}"

sdk_code_examples.title: Verify webhook signature
Verify webhook signature (PHP)
sdk_code_examples.code:
<?php

require __DIR__ . '/vendor/autoload.php';

use Acme\Client;
use Acme\Webhooks;

$client = new Client('sk_test_123');
$payload = file_get_contents('php://input');
$signature = $_SERVER['HTTP_ACME_SIGNATURE'] ?? '';

try {
    $event = Webhooks::verifySignature($payload, $signature, 'whsec_123');
    error_log('Received event ' . $event->type);
    http_response_code(200);
} catch (Exception $e) {
    http_response_code(400);
}

sdk_code_examples.title: List paginated pending orders
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

orders = client.orders.list_orders(
    status="pending",
    per_page=50,
    page=3,
    sort="-created_at",
)

for order in orders.data:
    print(order.id, order.status, order.created_at)

sdk_code_examples.title: List users with created_after filter
sdk_code_examples.code:
import acme_api

client = acme_api.Client(api_key="sk_test_123")

users = client.users.list_users(
    created_after="2023-01-01",
    per_page=25,
)

for user in users.data:
    print(user.id, user.email)

webhook_events.name: invoice.paid
webhook_events.description: Triggered when an invoice has been successfully paid and the payment is captured.

webhook_events.name: invoice.created
webhook_events.description: Occurs whenever a new invoice is created, before any payment attempt.

webhook_events.name: invoice.finalized
webhook_events.description: Sent when a draft invoice is finalized and ready to be sent or paid.

webhook_events.name: invoice.payment_failed
webhook_events.description: Sent when a payment attempt for an invoice fails or is declined.

webhook_events.name: invoice.voided
webhook_events.description: Triggered when an open or uncollectible invoice is marked as void.

webhook_events.name: payment.created
webhook_events.description: Occurs when a new payment object is created in the system.

webhook_events.name: payment.succeeded
webhook_events.description: Sent when a payment is successfully authorized and captured.

webhook_events.name: payment.failed
webhook_events.description: Triggered when a payment attempt fails due to an error or decline.

webhook_events.name: refund.created
webhook_events.description: Occurs whenever a refund is created for a charge or payment.

webhook_events.name: customer.created
webhook_events.description: Fires whenever a new customer record is created.

webhook_events.name: customer.updated
webhook_events.description: Sent when an existing customer's details are updated.

webhook_events.name: customer.deleted
webhook_events.description: Occurs when a customer is soft-deleted or archived.

webhook_events.name: subscription.created
webhook_events.description: Triggered when a new subscription is created for a customer.

webhook_events.name: subscription.canceled
webhook_events.description: Sent when an active subscription is canceled, either immediately or at period end.

api_endpoints.name: Create Project
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new project with the provided name and optional metadata.

api_endpoints.name: List Projects
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of projects visible to the authenticated account.

api_endpoints.name: Retrieve Project
api_endpoints.description: Fetches the details for a single project by its ID.

api_endpoints.name: List Users
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of users, with optional filters such as creation date.

api_endpoints.name: Retrieve User
api_endpoints.description: Fetches a single user by ID.

api_endpoints.name: Create User
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new user with the specified attributes.

api_endpoints.name: List Orders
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of orders with filtering and sorting options.

api_endpoints.name: Retrieve Order
api_endpoints.description: Retrieves the details for a single order. In v2, additional related data can be included via the include query parameter.

api_endpoints.name: Create Order
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new order for the specified customer and line items.

api_endpoints.name: Create Payment
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new payment for an invoice or order and attempts to charge the customer.

api_endpoints.name: Retrieve Payment
api_endpoints.description: Retrieves a payment by its unique identifier.

api_endpoints.name: List Payments
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of payments with optional filters such as status and creation date.

api_endpoints.name: List Invoices
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of invoices for the authenticated account.

api_endpoints.name: Retrieve Invoice
api_endpoints.description: Retrieves a single invoice by its ID.

api_endpoints.name: Create Invoice
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new invoice for a customer with one or more line items.

api_endpoints.name: Delete Customer
api_endpoints.description: Deletes (or archives) a customer by ID. This action is typically exposed via SDK methods such as delete_customer(customer_id=...).

api_endpoints.name: List Customers
api_endpoints.description: Returns a paginated list of customers, optionally filtered by status or creation date.

api_endpoints.name: Create Customer
api_endpoints.description: Creates a new customer record with contact and billing information.

api_endpoints.name: List Webhook Endpoints
api_endpoints.description: Returns a list of configured webhook endpoints for the account.

api_endpoints.name: Rotate API Key
api_endpoints.description: Rotates the primary API key for the authenticated account. Typically used in conjunction with the authentication documentation examples.

api_endpoint_versions.description: List users with pagination and optional filters such as created_after, email, and status. Supports created_after and per_page parameters used in the Try-It console examples.
List orders with pagination, status filter, and sorting. Supports status, per_page, page, and sort (e.g., -created_at) query parameters used by the Try-It console.
Retrieve an order by ID. The v1 version returns core order fields and basic totals without expandable related collections.
Retrieve an order by ID with support for the include query parameter. The include parameter accepts values such as items to embed related order line items directly in the response.
Create a new project with a specified name and optional metadata. This is the endpoint used in the Node.js code sample for creating projects from the docs.
Retrieve a paginated list of projects, optionally filtered by status, creation date, or owner.
Retrieve a single project by its unique identifier. Returns core project metadata and timestamps.
Create a payment for a given amount, currency, and customer. This endpoint is subject to specific per-minute rate limits documented in the Rate Limits guide.
List payments with pagination and optional filters such as status, customer_id, and created_at range.
List invoices for the authenticated account with pagination and status filters.
Delete (or archive) a customer by ID. This endpoint is wrapped by the Python SDK delete_customer(customer_id=...) method shown in the SDK docs.
List customers with support for pagination and filtering by email, status, and creation date.
Create a new customer record with billing and contact details. Commonly used before creating subscriptions or invoices.
List configured webhook endpoints, including their URLs, enabled event types, and signing secrets.
Rotate the primary API key for the current account. Often referenced from the Authentication guide as part of key management workflows.

api_code_samples.code:
curl https://api.acme.com/payments \
  -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "amount": 4999,
    "currency": "usd",
    "customer_id": "cus_123456789"
  }'

api_parameters.name: created_after
api_parameters.description: Return only users created on or after this ISO 8601 date.

api_parameters.name: per_page
api_parameters.description: Number of users to return per page.

api_parameters.name: status
api_parameters.description: Filter orders by status. Use pending to list only pending orders.

api_parameters.name: per_page
api_parameters.description: Number of orders to return per page.

api_parameters.name: sort
api_parameters.description: Sort order for results. Use -created_at for descending by creation time.

api_parameters.name: id
api_parameters.description: Unique identifier of the order to retrieve.

api_parameters.name: id
api_parameters.description: Unique identifier of the order to retrieve.

api_parameters.name: include
api_parameters.description: Comma-separated list of related resources to include in the response. Use items to embed order line items.

api_parameters.name: name
api_parameters.description: Human-readable project name. This field appears in the JSON payload body in the code samples.

api_parameters.name: metadata
api_parameters.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata to attach to the project.

api_parameters.name: amount
api_parameters.description: Amount to charge in the smallest currency unit (for example, cents).

api_parameters.name: customer_id
api_parameters.description: Identifier of the customer to charge.

api_parameters.name: per_page
api_parameters.description: Number of payments to return per page.

pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page for the Acme API documentation, with quick links to API Reference, Guides, Webhooks, SDKs, and Authentication.

pages.name: API Reference
pages.description: Complete reference for all REST API endpoints, including Try-It consoles and language-specific code samples.

pages.name: Guides
pages.description: High-level conceptual documentation and how-to guides, including authentication, pagination, and rate limiting.

pages.name: Webhooks
pages.description: Overview of configuring webhooks, verifying signatures, and subscribing to different event groups such as invoices and payments.

pages.name: SDKs
pages.description: Overview of official Acme client libraries, including Python, Node.js, Java, Ruby, Go, and PHP SDKs.

pages.name: Authentication
pages.description: Details on authenticating with the Acme API using API keys, bearer tokens, and example cURL commands.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Background information about the Acme platform, its APIs, and support policies.

pages.name: Rate Limits
pages.description: Documentation of global and per-endpoint rate limits, including specific limits for POST /payments and other write operations.

pages.name: Changelog
pages.description: History of API, SDK, and documentation changes across versions.

pages.name: Support
pages.description: How to get help, contact support, and report issues with the Acme API.

pages.name: FAQ
pages.description: Frequently asked questions and short answers about authentication, rate limits, and common integration issues.

pages.name: Invoice Events
pages.description: Detailed documentation for invoice-related webhook events such as invoice.paid, invoice.created, and invoice.payment_failed, including example JSON payloads and copy buttons.

pages.name: Payment Events
pages.description: Documentation for payment-related webhook events like payment.succeeded and payment.failed.

pages.name: Python SDK
pages.description: Reference for the Python SDK, including the Customers client and delete_customer(customer_id=...) method with example code and copy controls.

pages.name: Node.js SDK
pages.description: Reference for the Node.js SDK with examples for projects, users, and orders.

api_resources.name: Projects
api_resources.description: Endpoints for creating and managing projects, including POST /projects used in the Node.js code sample.

api_resources.name: Users
api_resources.description: Endpoints for listing and managing users, including GET /users with created_after and per_page filters.

api_resources.name: Orders
api_resources.description: Endpoints for listing and retrieving orders, including GET /orders and GET /orders/{id} with versioned behavior.

api_resources.name: Payments
api_resources.description: Endpoints for creating and listing payments, including POST /payments which has a documented per-minute rate limit.

api_resources.name: Invoices
api_resources.description: Endpoints for listing and retrieving invoices referenced by invoice-related webhooks.

api_resources.name: Customers
api_resources.description: Endpoints for creating, listing, and deleting customers, including DELETE /customers/{id} used by the Python SDK delete_customer method.

api_resources.name: Webhook Endpoints
api_resources.description: Endpoints for managing webhook endpoint configurations for receiving events.

api_resources.name: API Keys
api_resources.description: Endpoints for rotating and managing API keys, referenced from the Authentication guide.

rate_limit_rules.description: Default global rate limit of 600 requests per minute across all endpoints, unless overridden by a more specific rule.
Soft limit of 10 requests per second per API key.
POST /payments is limited to 120 requests per minute per account to protect payment processing systems.
GET /payments supports up to 300 read requests per minute.
GET /users is limited to 300 requests per minute per API key.
GET /orders is limited to 300 requests per minute per API key.
GET /orders/{id} is limited to 5 requests per second to prevent abusive polling.
POST /projects supports up to 240 project creation requests per minute.

sdks.name: Python SDK
sdks.description: Official Python client library for the Acme API, including a Customers client with a delete_customer(customer_id=...) method and comprehensive examples.

sdks.name: Node.js SDK
sdks.description: Official Node.js client library for the Acme API, with helpers for working with projects, users, orders, and payments.

webhook_event_groups.name: Invoice events

webhook_event_groups.name: Payment events

webhook_event_groups.name: Customer events
webhook_event_groups.description: Customer lifecycle webhook events, including customer.created, customer.updated, and customer.deleted.

webhook_event_groups.name: Subscription events

sdk_modules.name: Customers
sdk_modules.description: Manage customer records, including create, retrieve, list, update, and delete_customer(customer_id=...).

sdk_modules.name: Orders
sdk_modules.description: Create and manage orders, including listing orders and retrieving individual orders.

sdk_modules.name: Payments
sdk_modules.description: Create and retrieve payments associated with invoices or orders.

sdk_modules.name: Invoices
sdk_modules.description: Work with invoices, including listing and retrieving invoice objects.

sdk_modules.name: Projects
sdk_modules.description: Create and manage project resources via the Python client.

sdk_modules.name: Webhooks
sdk_modules.description: Helpers for validating and parsing incoming webhook events.

sdk_modules.name: Projects
sdk_modules.description: Project-related methods for the Node.js SDK, including creating projects from the documentation examples.

sdk_modules.name: Users
sdk_modules.description: List and manage users using the Node.js client.

sdk_modules.name: Orders
sdk_modules.description: Create and retrieve orders from Node.js applications.

sdk_modules.name: Payments
sdk_modules.description: Create and list payments with the Node.js SDK.

sdk_methods.name: delete_customer
sdk_methods.description: Deletes (archives) a customer by ID. This method maps to the DELETE /customers/{id} endpoint and is demonstrated in the Python SDK docs.

sdk_methods.name: get_customer
sdk_methods.description: Retrieves a single customer by ID and returns the full customer object.

sdk_methods.name: list_customers
sdk_methods.description: Lists customers with pagination and optional filters.

sdk_methods.name: create_customer
sdk_methods.description: Creates a new customer with email, name, and optional billing details.

sdk_methods.name: get_order
sdk_methods.description: Retrieves an order by ID. In newer API versions the underlying endpoint supports the include=items query parameter.

sdk_methods.name: list_orders
sdk_methods.description: Lists orders with support for status, pagination, and sorting parameters.

sdk_methods.name: create_project
sdk_methods.description: Creates a new project by calling POST /projects with a name and optional metadata. Used in the editable Node.js and Python code samples on the docs.

sdk_methods.name: get_project
sdk_methods.description: Retrieves a single project by its unique identifier.

sdk_methods.name: list_users
sdk_methods.description: Lists users with optional filters such as created_after and per_page. This method is shown in both Node.js and Python examples.

sdk_methods.name: create_payment
sdk_methods.description: Creates a payment for a specific customer and amount by wrapping the POST /payments endpoint, which has a documented per-minute rate limit.

sdk_methods.name: get_payment
sdk_methods.description: Retrieves a payment by its ID, returning status, amount, and related invoice or order information.

sdk_methods.name: get_invoice
sdk_methods.description: Retrieves a single invoice by ID. Often used together with invoice-related webhooks such as invoice.paid.

sdk_methods.name: verify_signature
sdk_methods.description: Verifies the signature of an incoming webhook payload using the configured signing secret. Used in Go and PHP examples to validate webhook events before processing.

auth_code_examples.title: Authenticate with API key using Bearer token (cURL)
Authenticate with API key (Node.js)
Authenticate with API key (Python requests)
Authenticate with API key (Ruby Net::HTTP)
auth_code_examples.code:
require 'net/http'
require 'json'

uri = URI('https://api.acme.com/orders')
req = Net::HTTP::Get.new(uri)
req['Authorization'] = 'Bearer <api_key>'
req['Accept'] = 'application/json'

res = Net::HTTP.start(uri.hostname, uri.port, use_ssl: true) do |http|
  http.request(req)
end

orders = JSON.parse(res.body)
puts orders['data'].map { |o| o['id'] }.join(', ')

auth_code_examples.title: Authenticate with API key (Go net/http)
Authenticate with API key (Java HttpClient)

auth_methods.name: API Key / Bearer Token
auth_methods.description: Use your secret API key as a bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <api_key>) for all requests. This method is shown in the cURL, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, and Java examples.

auth_methods.name: OAuth 2.0 Access Token
auth_methods.description: Authenticate using an OAuth 2.0 access token obtained via the OAuth flow. The access token is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

auth_methods.name: API Key via HTTP Basic Auth
auth_methods.description: Authenticate by sending your API key as the HTTP Basic Auth username (and an empty password). This is equivalent in privileges to using the API key as a bearer token.
